name: Centenary Oaks Trail
name_original: Los Robles Centenarios – circular desde La Robla
description: Hike the moderate 9.8-mile Los Robles Centenarios circular trail from La Robla, featuring ancient oak trees and historical sights.
region_intro: Hiking around La Robla offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain with panoramic views to dense forests and river valleys. The region features significant elevation changes, ancient oak and beech forests, and natural waterfalls. Trails often follow river courses, such as the Torío river, and incorporate historical elements like old mining paths and Roman bridges. This varied topography provides a range of hiking experiences for different skill levels. Best hiking trails in La Robla The most popular hiking route is Mina Bardaya Greenway, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route follows an old mining train path, featuring historical landmarks like the Roman Bridge of Villalfeide. Another top favourite among local hikers is Centenary Oaks Trail, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail highlights ancient oak trees, providing a connection to historical natural landscapes. Local hikers also love the Pico del Águila, Faedo de Orzonga y Refugio Cimero – circular desde Solana de Fenar, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the Faedo de Orzonaga beech forest, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around La Robla is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, river valleys, and historical greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes with considerable elevation gain. The routes in La Robla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Robla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Robla?
A: La Robla offers a diverse network of nearly 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 10 easy, 23 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in La Robla?
A: Hiking in La Robla reveals a rich tapestry of natural landscapes. You'll encounter mountainous terrain with panoramic views, dense forests featuring ancient oak trees on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041126413/centenary-oaks-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Oaks Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and beautiful beech forests such as the Faedo de Orzonaga. Many routes also follow picturesque river valleys, including the Torío river, and feature natural waterfalls.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the hiking routes in La Robla?
A: Yes, La Robla's hiking trails are rich in history. You can explore old mining paths, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051928680/mina-bardaya-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina Bardaya Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which features historical landmarks such as the Roman Bridge of Villalfeide and a monument to the miner. Other routes may lead to historical sights, trenches from past conflicts, or even the ruins of Alba Castle. The region is also part of the Camino del Salvador, a historic pilgrimage route.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Robla?
A: Yes, La Robla offers several circular hiking routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051918100/pico-del-aguila-faedo-de-orzonga-y-refugio-cimero-circular-desde-solana-de-fenar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila, Faedo de Orzonga y Refugio Cimero – circular desde Solana de Fenar&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the beautiful Faedo de Orzonaga beech forest. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051293060/pico-bustillo-loop-from-los-barrios-de-gordon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Bustillo Loop from Los Barrios de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramic views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in the La Robla area?
A: Yes, the La Robla region features trails with beautiful water features. You can find natural waterfalls, such as 'el salto' on the Ruta a Fontañan, and a small waterfall near a Roman bridge along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051928680/mina-bardaya-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina Bardaya Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt; is another notable highlight in the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Robla?
A: The hiking routes in La Robla are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from mountainous vistas to serene forest paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical elements.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in La Robla?
A: Yes, La Robla has 10 easy hiking trails that are generally suitable for families. These routes often feature gentler terrain and less elevation gain, allowing for a pleasant outdoor experience for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051928680/mina-bardaya-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina Bardaya Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate, is relatively flat and follows an old railway line, making it accessible for many.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in La Robla?
A: While many trails in La Robla are generally dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Robla?
A: La Robla offers great hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn are particularly vibrant, with the Faedo de Orzonaga beech forest displaying magnificent colors. Summer provides warmer weather for longer days on the trails, while winter can offer unique snowy landscapes, though some higher routes may require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any refuges or resting points along the longer trails?
A: Yes, for longer treks, you might encounter refuges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051918100/pico-del-aguila-faedo-de-orzonga-y-refugio-cimero-circular-desde-solana-de-fenar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila, Faedo de Orzonga y Refugio Cimero – circular desde Solana de Fenar&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, includes the Refugio Cimero, which provides a natural setting for hikers to rest and recover energy.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to explore near La Robla?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, La Robla is surrounded by impressive natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Nice viewpoint to the town of La Robla and Cueto San Mateo. We will find an information panel.
Going up the main path, an informative sign will divert us from the path to the left to follow a path between hundred-year-old oaks that could be more than 250 years old.
Beautiful views towards the Hoces and Pico Polvoreda from the picturesque path that leads to the town of La Robla.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Los Robles Centenarios trail?
faqs.answer: The Los Robles Centenarios trail is generally considered easy, making it suitable for most hikers, including families. While komoot classifies it as moderate due to its length, the terrain primarily consists of wide, simple forest tracks with no significant inclines.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 15.7 km (9.8 miles) long and typically takes around 4 hours and 20 minutes to complete at a steady hiking pace.

faqs.question: Where is the recommended starting point for the trail?
faqs.answer: To enhance your hiking experience and avoid urban sections, it's recommended to start from a viewpoint in the Rabizo area, rather than the town center of La Robla. This alternative starting point is near the water deposits and the Cecinas Ezequiel warehouse.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied landscapes, including grassy paths, wide forest tracks, and narrower, winding trails. You'll primarily walk through pine forests, with a spectacular section winding through the ancient centenary oaks. Some parts run parallel to roads, and there's a subterranean tunnel under a highway.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the ancient Ancient Oak Trees, which give the route its name, and the Viewpoint of La Robla. You'll also pass the historic Ermita de Celada and enjoy views of Pico Polvoreda.

faqs.question: Is the Los Robles Centenarios trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through areas with other hikers or wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter might bring cooler weather but also unique views.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Los Robles Centenarios trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Los Robles Centenarios trail?
faqs.answer: If you choose the recommended starting point near the viewpoint in the Rabizo area, there should be parking available. Specific parking details for this alternative start are best checked locally upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like picnic areas or playgrounds along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a designated recreational area along the route. It offers benches, picnic tables, and a playground, making it a great spot for a break or a family outing.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Los Robles Centenarios trail, officially known as PRC-LE 74, is a well-marked circular route. It also shares sections with other paths, including the Ruta de los robles centenarios (100%), Camino del Salvador (18%), and Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón (7%).

faqs.question: Is the Los Robles Centenarios trail suitable for cycling?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is 100% cyclable, making it suitable for mountain biking as well as hiking. The wide forest tracks are particularly good for bikes.
